title: Why I Want to Go From Lean FIRE to Regular FIRE (and How I Plan To)
meta_title: Why I Want to Go From Lean FIRE to Regular FIRE
subtitle: Two ideas that changed how I think about money and time, and the plan that follows from them
description: Once your investments grow past a point, compounding does more than saving ever can. Here is why I want to move from Lean FIRE to Regular FIRE, and how I plan to get there without trading my time for a salary.

faqs.question: Why go from Lean FIRE to Regular FIRE?
faqs.answer: Because your wants change as your life moves forward, and once your floor is secure you can chase a fuller life with almost no risk. For me it is about more permanence and the freedom to live comfortably anywhere, all built on top of a lean base that already covers me.

faqs.question: At what point does compounding matter more than saving?
faqs.answer: Once your portfolio grows past a certain size, the yearly growth on what you already own becomes larger than what you could add by saving more. After that point your main job is to protect the pile and let it compound, rather than to keep feeding it.

faqs.question: Why stop earning a regular salary after Lean FIRE?
faqs.answer: A salary is safe, but it is capped, since you trade fixed hours for a fixed amount of money. Once your investments grow on their own and your floor is covered, a better use of your time is putting it into ideas with a real shot at very big growth, where the downside is small and the upside stays open.

faqs.question: How do you reach a bigger FIRE number without a salary?
faqs.answer: You let your existing investments compound untouched, you keep living at your lean level so you avoid lifestyle creep, and you spend your free time on high upside bets and the occasional high paying work. The base grows on its own while you swing for the wins on top.

faqs.question: Is it risky to chase more after reaching Lean FIRE?
faqs.answer: Not if you protect your floor. Keep the money that guarantees your freedom completely safe and never gamble with it. Take your risks only with your time and small amounts, so a failed bet costs you effort rather than your security.

cover_alt: Thinking about moving from Lean FIRE to Regular FIRE
